title: PagerDuty Incident Volume Spike vs 4-Week Baseline per Service
app: PagerDuty App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3013) or Events API v2 scripted input
spl:
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by service_name, _time
| eventstats median(count) as med by service_name
| where count > med*3 AND count > 10
| sort -count
description: Sudden paging storms indicate brittle monitors or upstream outages.
value: Separates real incidents from alert noise using service-level baselines.
implementation: Backfill 28d for `med`; exclude maintenance windows via `suppressor` field if present.
knownFalsePositives: Real outages, wide dependency failure, and new or noisy monitor rules; check the same service in the on-call product before you blame a single team.
grandmaExplanation: We compare this week’s paging to a few weeks of normal, so a single noisy service or a real storm stands out from everyday bumps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure: PagerDuty App for Splunk (Splunkbase 3013) or Events API v2 scripted input.
• Data: PagerDuty incidents API or webhook (`pagerduty:incident`).
• For directory layout and inputs, see docs/implementation-guide.md.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Backfill 28d for `med`; exclude maintenance windows via `suppressor` field if present.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following search (then save as report or alert; tune time range and threshold):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype="pagerduty:incident"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by service_name, _time
| eventstats median(count) as med by service_name
| where count > med*3 AND count > 10
| sort -count
```

Understanding this search
Sudden paging storms indicate brittle monitors or upstream outages.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PagerDuty incident timeline and service view for the same window. Reconcile a few incident numbers, state changes, and acknowledgement times. If anything disagrees, check export lag, time zone, and which API fields we map.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add to a dashboard, wire alert actions, and document ownership. Suggested view: Line chart (count vs baseline), Table (service).
